intro:
I couldn't find any information on Fagelia montana. However, I found information on Fagelia, which is also known as Fagara. Fagara is a genus of plants in the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. Here is a paragraph about Fagara:

Fagara, also known as Fagelia, is a genus of plants in the Rutaceae family, commonly used as a spice or medicinal herb. Fagara has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Fagara include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Fagara include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
